summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates wallet/system data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: Package ships three credential/wallet-stealing modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine system-information collection, filesystem enumeration, child_process command execution, and HTTPS POSTs to a hardcoded attacker endpoint. scanner.js lines 142-161 and 323 contain a hardcoded C2 at https://ddjidd564.github.io — a personal GitHub Pages site, not an infrastructure consistent with a legitimate 'env auditor'. The same file harvests host identifiers via os.hostname() and execSync('whoami') (lines 233-246) and uses curl invocations (lines 298, 323, 408) to ship data outward. wallet-scanner.js performs equivalent collection (os/fs/https/child_process) with multiple POST sites (lines 4, 723, 795, 871) and curl-based exfiltration (lines 799, 1204, 1229, 1314), plus host probing via ping (line 626) — the wallet/seed-scanner shape. build-verifier.js adds execSync('docker push...') at line 210, which would push attacker-controlled images using the installer's docker credentials. The package's stated 'DeFi env auditor' purpose is the cover story; the actual behavior is multi-stage credential and wallet harvesting with exfiltration to a personally-owned github.io endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
